content.full_text:
April 3, 1991

Mr. Mark Morodomi  
428 J St., Ste. 600  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

Per our telephone conversation on March 29, 1991, I am writing requesting a written confirmation of our discussion. I have been asked by a member of the Huntington Beach City Council to serve on the Huntington Beach Planning Commission and need to know whether or not I have any conflict of interest due to the fact that I lease office space from the Koll Co., a major developer in Orange County. The Koll Co. specializes in office buildings, but has recently taken over the management of a large development in the City of Huntington Beach, which will be residential. In the future the Koll Co. will bring this project before the Planning Commission, and I will want to be able to vote on the project. I have contacted the Warner Newhope Associates who manage the building for the Koll Co. and Cigna Associates, and have been informed that my partner and I pay the same rate as other tenants in the building, and is the same rate paid throughout Orange County. All discussions regarding our lease were conducted by my partner and all discussions regarding the property are done with Warner Newhope Associates. I have no monetary advantage or relationship with Koll at this time, nor will I have any monetary relationship with any of the Koll properties, now or in the future.

My appointment to the Council will take effect on April 29, 1990, and I would appreciate a written statement as soon as possible. If I can answer any additional questions please contact me at 714-966-2469. I have conveyed our telephone conversation to the City Council where you thought that since I did not have a monetary interest in Koll, I would be free to act as a Planning Commissioner.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Shirley Dettloff
